items.a: We provide structured support for work permits, residence permits, and employer-side hiring processes in Georgia. We focus on a clear start, correct procedure selection, document review, and practical guidance through key steps.

items.q: Do I actually need a work permit in my case?
items.a: Not every situation is the same. Whether you need a work permit depends on your status and how you work in Georgia. We assess your case and explain the correct route before you invest time and effort into the wrong process.

items.q: What is the most common mistake people make at the start?
items.a: Starting without clarity: choosing the wrong route, preparing unnecessary documents, or missing mandatory steps and deadlines. Our role is to turn uncertainty into a structured plan of action.

items.q: Can you help with residence permits based on different grounds?
items.a: Yes. A residence permit requires a specific legal basis. We help identify the best-fitting basis for your case, align your documents, and organise the process correctly from the start.

items.q: I already started the process. Can you review what I’ve done?
items.a: Yes. If you’ve already begun, we can review your current stage, check documents, identify risks or gaps, and recommend the next steps to stabilise the process.
Yes. We help companies organise the hiring process for foreign employees as a controlled business workflow—reducing confusion, delays, and compliance risk, and easing pressure on HR and operations.

items.q: What does an initial assessment include?
items.a: A focused review of your situation: what route applies, what you need to prepare, what to avoid, and what the next steps look like. The goal is a clear plan, not generic advice.
We treat each case confidentially and keep communication clear and structured. We only request what’s necessary to assess and support your process.

items.q: How long does the process take?
items.a: Timelines depend on the route and your readiness. During the initial assessment we outline the realistic sequence and what typically affects timing in your case.
Use Call Us | WhatsApp | Email Us and we’ll respond with the next step for an initial assessment.

hero.slogan: Professional Work, Residence and Employer Support in Georgia

work-permit.title: Work Permit
work-permit.short: If you are a foreign national, work as an individual entrepreneur (IE), or plan to start working or doing business in Georgia, we help determine whether a work permit is required and how to start the process correctly.
work-permit.description: If you work as an IE, plan to start a business or work in Georgia, intend to take up employment, or as an employer plan to hire a foreign employee, it is important from the outset to understand whether a work permit is required in your case and which procedure applies. This is where we step in: we analyse your situation, identify the right route and provide clear steps for starting the process correctly.
work-permit.description2: This service is especially useful if you are uncertain about your status, don't know which documents will be needed, aren't sure where to begin, or have already started the process and want to make sure you are moving in the right direction.

residence-permit.title: Residence Permit
residence-permit.short: We help determine the legal basis for a residence permit in Georgia, prepare the right documents, and start the process without unnecessary mistakes or delays.
residence-permit.description: A residence permit in Georgia is not issued simply on request — it requires a specific legal basis. The most common mistake at the start is choosing the wrong basis, which leads to lost time, unnecessary documents, or a delayed application. We help you determine from the outset whether you have a suitable basis, which application route fits your case, and how to organise the process correctly.

employer-support.title: Employer Support
employer-support.short: We help companies build a structured process for hiring foreign employees in Georgia — without confusion, missed deadlines, or unnecessary business risk.
employer-support.heroShort: Hiring process, compliance and ongoing support.
employer-support.description: For employers, hiring a foreign national is not just about the hire itself — it is about organising the process correctly from the start. In practice, most problems arise at this stage: unclear steps, incorrect sequencing, missed deadlines, and the lack of a structured process, which creates unnecessary pressure on HR, management, and operations teams.
employer-support.description2: We help companies turn this into a clear and manageable process. We analyse your specific case, identify the correct procedure, review documents, and support you at key stages — so that hiring foreign employees becomes a controlled business process rather than a chaotic task.

about.label: About Us
about.body1: Founded in 2014, Stay & Work Georgia provides professional support to foreign nationals and employers navigating work permits, residence permits and related employment processes in Georgia.
about.body2: We approach each case with clarity, careful preparation and structured support — from initial assessment and document review to filing guidance and follow-up.

footer.description: We help foreign nationals navigate work permits, residence permits and business setup in Georgia.
